step.title: Generate HTTP Middleware goal (conditional, phase=middleware)
step.rule: Fixtures goal (phase=fixtures) MUST exist in goals.yaml before this step runs.
This step is CONDITIONAL — if no middleware was selected during discovery, skip entirely with a log message and proceed to the next step.
All inputs read from docs/architecture/* files on disk (G-16), never from conversation context.

substep.title: Check middleware selection in cross-cutting.md
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md. Find the middleware section (written by discover.xml Step 4.2/6.4 — covers CORS, request logging, rate limiting).
Extract which middleware features were selected/enabled. Build SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE list.

branch.condition: SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE is empty — no middleware selected
branch.action: Print: "No HTTP middleware selected — skipping Step 3.22"
SKIP — proceed to next step. Do NOT call goal-create.

substep.title: Resolve dependency goal IDs
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Find the goal with phase=fixtures — store its ID as FIXTURES_GOAL_ID.

check.action: ABORT with error: "Cannot generate middleware goal: no fixtures goal (phase=fixtures) found in goals.yaml — ensure fixtures step ran first"

substep.title: Derive the coarse deliverable_area
substep.action: Set MIDDLEWARE_AREA = the middleware deliverable directory in the resolved topology — projects/{app}/src/Http/Middleware/ (the cross-cutting middleware directory the shard's old context/quality-gates referenced, where the kernel event subscribers/listeners live; deployable-app layer, not per-BC), where {app} is the deployable name from the resolved conventions/topology. This is a COARSE path (the depinfer produce/consume seed + the elaborator's tree-read anchor), NOT a glob and NOT a per-file path.
substep.rule: Do NOT read task-plan-spec criteria, compose validate commands, or author any acceptance/validate/investigator/not-in-scope here — those are Tier-2 (/tmux:elaborate authors them at dispatch, against SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE). The roadmap skeleton commits only to the coarse deliverable_area.

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP (roadmap skeleton)
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with a roadmap SKELETON:

params.param: Configure HTTP middleware: {{SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE}} with kernel registration and tests (≤120 chars — truncate list if needed)

substep.rule: Description dynamically lists selected middleware names — must stay ≤120 chars (MaxGoalDescriptionLength). If list exceeds limit, abbreviate: "Configure HTTP middleware: N features with kernel registration".
max_retries=3 — middleware goals have focused cross-cutting scope.

NO validate/acceptance/scope/context/investigation_config/not_in_scope params — this is a roadmap skeleton; /tmux:elaborate authors every concrete field at dispatch (design §5/§6). NEVER run `tmux-cli rules match` here.

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Print: "Middleware skeleton created: goal-{{ID}} (phase=middleware, status=roadmap, depends_on=[{{FIXTURES_GOAL_ID}}], deliverable_area={{MIDDLEWARE_AREA}}, selected: {{SELECTED_MIDDLEWARE}})"
